pattern: Repeatedly summing decimal digits converges to the digital root, which has the closed form based on congruence modulo nine.
hints: Summing digits repeatedly until one digit remains is the classic digital-root operation.
A number and the sum of its digits leave the same remainder when divided by nine, so the final value depends only on that remainder.
For a positive number the answer is 1 + (num - 1) mod 9, and zero maps to zero; that needs no loop at all.

edge_inputs.description: nine maps to itself, the only multiple-of-nine root
multiple of nine collapses to nine, not zero

stress.description: uniformly random non-negative integers within the 32-bit range
stress.note: size is ignored; covers the full value domain

stress.description: exact multiples of nine, where a naive digit-sum-to-zero bug shows up
stress.note: every input has digital root nine
